task_list.name: bus_transport_search
task_list.text: Find the cheapest bus (e.g., FlixBus) or carpooling (e.g., BlaBlaCar) options from Stuttgart to Prague for June 20th and returning June 23rd, 2026 for a solo traveler.

task_list.name: hostel_dorm_search
task_list.text: Find the cheapest shared hostel dorms in Prague for 3 nights (June 20-23, 2026) within walking distance of the Old Town to minimize local transport costs.

task_list.name: free_attractions_prague
task_list.text: Identify free or very low-cost historical attractions in Prague's Old Town and surrounding areas, such as Charles Bridge, Old Town Square, and free walking tours.

task_list.name: street_food_recommendations
task_list.text: Find budget-friendly street food spots and cheap local eateries in Prague to fit a tight daily budget, focusing on local specialties like Trdelník or Chlebíčky.

task_list.name: attraction_hours_check
task_list.text: Verify the opening hours for major free landmarks in Prague like Charles Bridge and the Old Town Square for June 2026.

task_list.name: itinerary_routing_check
task_list.text: Check the walking distances between the selected hostel, street food spots, and free attractions to ensure no public transport costs are needed.

task_list.name: budget_feasibility_check
task_list.text: Calculate the total estimated cost of transport, accommodation, and food to ensure the entire trip for the solo traveler stays under the €250 limit.

travelplan.title: Stuttgart to Prague ultra-budget solo trip, 20–23 June 2026

slots.name: Target carpool: Stuttgart → Prague Florenc
slots.description: Budget target: book a BlaBlaCar seat 2–14 days before departure; typical duration about 5h49 and estimated seat price €19–€29. Fixed backup: early-book FlixBus from Stuttgart Airport/North to Prague Florenc, usually €32.50+ one-way. Time is a practical planning window; confirm the exact driver or bus time when booking.
slots.notes: Avoid paid seat reservations and extra luggage; use one small backpack.

slots.name: Walk Florenc bus station → Hostel Florenc
slots.description: Free 5-minute walk, about 331–359 m, from ÚAN Florenc to the hostel on Pod výtopnou.
slots.notes: No public transport ticket needed.

slots.name: Check in: Hostel Florenc shared dorm
slots.description: Shared dorm base next to Florenc bus station; live hotel search found Sir Toby’s at €31/night, while Hostel Florenc is the more location-efficient target near the arrival terminal. Budget assumes €31/night x 3 nights plus local tax separately.
slots.notes: Book direct/early if Hostel Florenc dorm is cheaper; keep target under €31/night.

slots.name: Prague city tax reserve
slots.description: Reserve for local stay tax, typically about 50 CZK / €2 per person per night, payable at hostel check-in.
slots.notes: 3 nights x approx. €2.

slots.name: Cheap transit lunch: bakery/supermarket food
slots.description: Buy rolls, cheese, fruit, or a chlebíček-style snack after arrival instead of eating in the tourist core.
slots.location: Florenc / Na Poříčí supermarket or bakery
slots.notes: Keep lunch under 125 CZK.

slots.name: Walk Hostel Florenc → Old Town Square
slots.description: Free walk of about 1.4–1.5 km, typically 16–17 minutes; buffer added for orientation and cobblestones.
slots.notes: Avoid metro fare unless weather is bad.

slots.name: Old Town Square + Astronomical Clock exterior
slots.description: Free first look at Prague’s historical core: Old Town Square, Týn Church exterior, Astronomical Clock hourly show. Public square is open 24/7; clock show runs hourly from 09:00 to 23:00.
slots.notes: Skip paid Old Town Hall Tower to protect the €250 budget.

slots.name: Cheap dinner: chlebíčky / street-food style meal
slots.description: Choose budget sandwiches, bakery food, or a small Czech takeaway meal near Old Town/Florenc; avoid tourist-priced trdelník stands on Old Town Square.
slots.location: Old Town / Florenc budget bakery or deli
slots.notes: Target 200 CZK or less.

slots.name: Charles Bridge sunset walk
slots.description: Walk the Royal Way from Old Town Square to Charles Bridge; distance about 600–700 m / 8–12 minutes. Charles Bridge is free and open 24/7.
slots.notes: Bridge towers are paid; skip them.

slots.name: Return walk to hostel
slots.description: Walk back toward Florenc through Old Town/New Town, keeping the day transport cost at €0 in Prague.
slots.notes: Use well-lit central streets.

days.label: Old Town, Jewish Quarter + Castle viewpoints

slots.name: Hostel/simple breakfast
slots.description: Use hostel breakfast if included; otherwise buy supermarket rolls/yogurt/fruit.
slots.notes: Target 100 CZK or less.

slots.name: Walk to free walking tour meeting area
slots.description: Walk from Florenc to Old Town Square/Powder Tower area. The hostel-to-Old-Town walk is under 20 minutes; arrive early for a pre-booked tip-based tour.
slots.location: Hostel Florenc → Old Town / Powder Tower
slots.notes: Reserve online 1–2 days before; choose English.

slots.name: Tip-based Old Town + Jewish Quarter walking tour
slots.description: Tip-based historical walk covering Old Town, Jewish Quarter exteriors, Charles Bridge context, Astronomical Clock, and medieval lanes. No upfront fee; budget a modest €10 tip.
slots.notes: Do not enter paid synagogues/cemetery on this ultra-budget version.

slots.name: Budget lunch: supermarket picnic / chlebíčky
slots.description: Refuel with deli sandwiches/chlebíčky, bakery items, or supermarket picnic food instead of a sit-down restaurant in Prague 1.
slots.location: Old Town / Josefov budget deli or supermarket
slots.notes: Target 175 CZK or less.

slots.name: Prague Castle courtyards + St. Vitus exterior/free nave
slots.description: Explore the free castle courtyards and viewpoints; castle grounds usually open 06:00–22:00, historical buildings 09:00–17:00 in summer. Skip the paid Castle Circuit; use the free exterior and limited cathedral nave access if available.
slots.notes: Security checks can add time.

slots.name: Nový Svět + Hradčany backstreets
slots.description: Free slow walk through the quieter New World/Hradčany lanes and gas-lamp area for medieval atmosphere away from the tourist core.
slots.notes: Good low-cost alternative to paid interiors.

slots.name: Cheap Czech pub dinner: U Černého vola
slots.description: Inexpensive traditional Czech pub near Hradčany; good fit after the castle walk. Keep order simple: one main or soup + bread, tap water if available.
slots.location: U Černého vola, Loretánské nám. 107/1, Prague
slots.notes: Open daily 10:00–22:00 per restaurant lookup.

slots.name: Downhill walk via Lesser Town + Lennon Wall
slots.description: Free downhill walk through Malá Strana, including exterior lanes and John Lennon Wall, then back toward Old Town/Florenc as energy allows.
slots.notes: Lennon Wall is a free public landmark.

slots.name: Return walk to Hostel Florenc
slots.description: Walk back via Old Town to keep local transit costs at zero.
slots.location: Malá Strana / Old Town → Hostel Florenc
slots.notes: If feet are done, use one PID 30-min ticket instead.

slots.name: Walk Charles Bridge → Prague Castle district
slots.description: Free uphill walk via Malá Strana/Nerudova or Old Castle Stairs. Charles Bridge to Castle is roughly 2.9 km from the Old Town side and can take up to ~34 minutes because of crowds and the climb.
slots.notes: If exhausted, Tram 22 is the fallback but costs a 30-min ticket.

slots.name: Hostel/simple breakfast
slots.description: Cheap hostel/supermarket breakfast: rolls, yogurt, fruit, or included hostel breakfast if available.
slots.notes: Keep under 100 CZK.

slots.name: Walk Hostel Florenc → Vítkov Hill
slots.description: Walk from Florenc/Karlín up toward Vítkov; free local transit and a useful viewpoint route.
slots.notes: Expect an uphill section.

slots.name: Vítkov Hill + National Monument exterior
slots.description: Free historical viewpoint over Žižkov and Karlín, with the Jan Žižka equestrian statue and National Monument exterior. The outdoor park/viewpoint costs nothing; skip paid museum interiors.
slots.notes: Good contrast to touristy Old Town.

slots.name: Budget lunch: Karlín bakery / supermarket picnic
slots.description: Use a bakery or supermarket in Karlín/Florenc for a cheap picnic lunch rather than a tourist restaurant.
slots.notes: Target 175 CZK or less.

slots.name: Free self-guided New Town walk
slots.description: Walk from Florenc toward Republic Square, Municipal House exterior, Powder Tower exterior, and Wenceslas Square. All exterior sightseeing is free; skip tower/museum tickets.
slots.notes: Powder Tower entry is paid, but exterior is free.

slots.name: Letná Park free viewpoint walk
slots.description: Free riverside/park walk to Letná for classic Vltava bridge views without paying for a tower viewpoint.
slots.notes: Bring water from supermarket.

slots.name: 30-minute PID ticket reserve to Vyšehrad area
slots.description: Use one short Prague public transport ticket if needed to reach Vyšehrad/Pod Vyšehradem without over-walking. Official 2026 30-min ticket: 36 CZK in PID Lítačka app, 39 CZK paper/contactless.
slots.notes: Walk instead if energy/weather allow.

slots.name: Cheap Czech dinner: Pod Vyšehradem
slots.description: Inexpensive Czech meal at a well-rated budget restaurant near Vyšehrad. Keep order simple to stay under budget.
slots.notes: Open Monday 11:00–22:00 per restaurant lookup.

slots.name: 30-minute PID ticket reserve back to Florenc
slots.description: Use a second short PID ticket back to Florenc/hostel if needed after the long day; walk only if comfortable.
slots.notes: This is the main local transport allowance of the trip.

slots.name: Vyšehrad ramparts sunset walk
slots.description: Free historical fort ramparts and river views around Vyšehrad after dinner; skip paid interiors.
slots.notes: Good value evening viewpoint.

slots.name: Hostel/simple breakfast
slots.description: Final cheap breakfast from hostel breakfast or supermarket/bakery food.
slots.notes: Keep under 100 CZK.

slots.name: Check out and store/keep backpack
slots.description: Check out by the hostel deadline. Avoid paid luggage storage if possible by carrying a small backpack; if needed, use hostel luggage room only if free.
slots.notes: Hostel Florenc/hostels often check out around 10:00–11:00; confirm exact time.

slots.name: Cheap lunch before departure
slots.description: Buy a final low-cost lunch from a bakery/supermarket near Florenc: rolls, chlebíčky, fruit, or a prepared deli item.
slots.location: Florenc / Na Poříčí bakery or supermarket
slots.notes: Target 175 CZK or less.

slots.name: Walk to ÚAN Florenc and check departure point
slots.description: Free short walk back to Prague’s main bus/carpool hub; arrive early enough to confirm platform or driver pickup point.
slots.notes: Florenc terminal is next to the hostel.

slots.name: Target carpool: Prague Florenc → Stuttgart
slots.description: Budget target: BlaBlaCar return seat, estimated €19–€29. Fixed backup: early FlixBus Prague→Stuttgart from around €26.50+ if booked early. Exact time must be confirmed once seats/tickets are released.
slots.notes: If carpool availability is weak, book FlixBus early; this plan still stays close to budget with cheapest bus fares.

slots.name: Packed dinner/snacks for the road
slots.description: Buy supermarket snacks/water before boarding so you do not need expensive station or rest-stop food.
slots.notes: Use remaining coins/CZK; target 200 CZK or less.

slots.name: Final free Old Town wander / souvenir-free morning
slots.description: Walk the free central route again: Powder Tower exterior, Municipal House exterior, Old Town lanes, and riverside if weather is good. No paid towers or museums.
slots.location: Florenc → Old Town / Na Příkopě / riverside
slots.notes: Use the morning for photos before crowds build.

Research budget transport, hostel, attractions, eateries, and key opening facts

Add transport, lodging, meals, and attraction slots within budget
Review cost summary and finalize concise trip summary
